statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a Git tag name. A valid Git tag name consists of only letters (both uppercase and lowercase), digits, and hyphens, starting with a letter or a digit. The name should not be longer than 200 characters. Your function should return `true` if the tag name is valid and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Git tag name.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the tag name is valid.
statement.notes: The function should be able to handle both ASCII and non-ASCII characters.

constraints.description: The maximum length of the tag name

examples.explanation: A valid Git tag name with digits and a hyphen.
A tag name with spaces is not valid.
A tag name with special characters is not valid.

hints.text: First hint text: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid Git tag name.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Validating Git Tag Names
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid Git tag name.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Iterate over each character in the tag name and check if it is a letter, digit, or hyphen.
editorial.betterSolution: A solution that uses a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid Git tag name.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid Git tag name and check the length of the tag name.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The regular expression matches the pattern of a valid Git tag name and the length check ensures the tag name does not exceed 200 characters.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Given a tag name, check if it matches the pattern of a valid Git tag name and check its length.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not checking the length of the tag name.
Mistake 2: Using a regular expression that is too permissive.
